name: Belém do Pará Convention (Inter-American Convention on the Prevention, Punishment and Eradication of Violence Against Women 1994)
summary: The Belém do Pará Convention (formally Inter-American Convention on the Prevention, Punishment and Eradication of Violence Against Women / Convención Interamericana para Prevenir, Sancionar y Erradicar la Violencia contra la Mujer) is a Latin American supranational meta-framework violence-against-women binding-treaty instrument adopted by the Organization of American States (OAS) — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern regional binding-treaty framework operating specifically on violence-against-women framework predating Istanbul Convention 2011 framework (Belém do Pará Convention adopted 9 June 1994 by OAS XXIV General Assembly in Belém do Pará, Brazil + effective 5 March 1995 — formal Latin American regional binding-treaty framework establishing comprehensive violence-against-women framework, distinct from Istanbul Convention 2011 framework adopted 17 years later, structurally distinctive globally as the first regional binding-treaty framework operating specifically on violence-against-women framework, predating Istanbul Convention 2011 framework by 17 years), as the central jurisdiction of the formal Inter-American post-CEDAW comprehensive-violence-against-women framework elaboration (Belém do Pará Convention adopted as Inter-American regional supplement to CEDAW 1979 framework — applicable to 32 of 34 OAS member states that have ratified the Convention as of 2026, with only United States + Cuba not ratifying framework, structurally distinctive globally as the most-widely-adopted regional binding-treaty framework on violence-against-women framework with near-universal Latin American + Caribbean adoption framework), as the central jurisdiction of one of the most distinctive modern Inter-American Court of Human Rights case-law frameworks (Inter-American Court of Human Rights IACtHR González et al. (Cotton Field) v. Mexico 16 November 2009 + Veliz Franco et al. v. Guatemala 19 May 2014 + multiple subsequent IACtHR cases — formal IACtHR case-law framework applying Belém do Pará Convention framework to state-party violations, distinct from rest-of-modern-regional-women's-rights frameworks operating without formal regional-court-case-law framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the MESECVI monitoring framework (Inter-American Mechanism for the Follow-up of the Convention on the Prevention, Punishment and Eradication of Violence Against Women MESECVI established 26 October 2004 — formal dedicated monitoring-body framework with formal periodic-evaluation + Country Report framework, distinct from CEDAW Committee framework + GREVIO framework). The Belém do Pará Convention is binding upon 32 of 34 OAS member states that have ratified the Convention as of 2026 (excluding United States + Cuba). The Convention is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal violence-against-women framework intersects with parental-alienation framework. The MESECVI is the apex monitoring framework body; the Inter-American Court of Human Rights is the apex judicial body. The Belém do Pará Convention is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.citation: Inter-American Convention on the Prevention, Punishment and Eradication of Violence Against Women 1994
statutory_framework.title: Belém do Pará Convention
statutory_framework.relevance: Inter-American Convention on the Prevention, Punishment and Eradication of Violence Against Women adopted 9 June 1994 by OAS XXIV General Assembly in Belém do Pará, Brazil + effective 5 March 1995 — formal Latin American regional binding-treaty framework establishing comprehensive violence-against-women framework.

statutory_framework.title: MESECVI Establishment Statute
statutory_framework.relevance: Inter-American Mechanism for the Follow-up of the Convention on the Prevention, Punishment and Eradication of Violence Against Women MESECVI established 26 October 2004 — formal dedicated monitoring-body framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Inter-American Court of Human Rights González et al. (Cotton Field) v. Mexico 2009 + Veliz Franco et al. v. Guatemala 2014
statutory_framework.title: IACtHR Cotton Field + Veliz Franco Cases
statutory_framework.relevance: Inter-American Court of Human Rights IACtHR González et al. (Cotton Field) v. Mexico 16 November 2009 + Veliz Franco et al. v. Guatemala 19 May 2014 — formal IACtHR case-law framework applying Belém do Pará Convention framework to state-party violations.

statutory_framework.title: CEDAW
statutory_framework.relevance: UN Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women CEDAW 1979/1981 — universal multilateral framework underlying Belém do Pará Convention framework.

apex_courts.name: Inter-American Mechanism for the Follow-up of the Convention on the Prevention, Punishment and Eradication of Violence Against Women (MESECVI)
apex_courts.role: Apex monitoring framework body for Belém do Pará Convention.

apex_courts.name: Inter-American Court of Human Rights (IACtHR)
apex_courts.role: Apex judicial body for state-party violations of Belém do Pará Convention + American Convention on Human Rights.

apex_courts.name: Inter-American Commission on Human Rights
apex_courts.role: Inter-American Commission on Human Rights — formal Inter-American human rights monitoring body, including Special Rapporteurship on the Rights of Women framework.

professional_regulators.name: OAS Secretariat
professional_regulators.role: Belém do Pará Convention implementation framework.

anonymisation_convention: Belém do Pará Convention-related individual communications are anonymised per MESECVI + IACtHR practice using initials.

key_developments.title: CEDAW adopted
key_developments.description: UN Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women CEDAW adopted 18 December 1979 — universal multilateral framework underlying Belém do Pará Convention.

key_developments.title: Belém do Pará Convention adopted
key_developments.description: Inter-American Convention on the Prevention, Punishment and Eradication of Violence Against Women adopted 9 June 1994 by OAS XXIV General Assembly in Belém do Pará, Brazil — first regional binding-treaty framework operating specifically on violence-against-women framework.

key_developments.title: Belém do Pará Convention enters force
key_developments.description: Inter-American Convention on the Prevention, Punishment and Eradication of Violence Against Women entered force 5 March 1995 — formal regional binding-treaty framework operational.

key_developments.title: MESECVI established
key_developments.description: Inter-American Mechanism for the Follow-up of the Convention on the Prevention, Punishment and Eradication of Violence Against Women MESECVI established 26 October 2004 — formal dedicated monitoring-body framework.

key_developments.title: Cotton Field judgment
key_developments.description: Inter-American Court of Human Rights IACtHR González et al. (Cotton Field) v. Mexico Judgment 16 November 2009 — landmark formal IACtHR case-law framework applying Belém do Pará Convention framework to Mexican state-party violations.

key_developments.title: Veliz Franco judgment
key_developments.description: Inter-American Court of Human Rights IACtHR Veliz Franco et al. v. Guatemala Judgment 19 May 2014 — formal IACtHR case-law framework applying Belém do Pará Convention framework to Guatemalan state-party violations.

structural_findings: Belém do Pará Convention operates a supranational Latin American regional violence-against-women binding-treaty framework — places Belém do Pará Convention in the Latin American supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern regional binding-treaty framework operating specifically on violence-against-women framework predating Istanbul Convention 2011 framework is structurally distinctive globally — first regional binding-treaty framework operating specifically on violence-against-women framework.
Central jurisdiction of formal Inter-American post-CEDAW comprehensive-violence-against-women framework elaboration is structurally distinctive globally — Belém do Pará Convention framework.

Central jurisdiction of one of most distinctive modern Inter-American Court of Human Rights case-law frameworks is structurally distinctive globally — Cotton Field + Veliz Franco + subsequent IACtHR cases.
Central jurisdiction of MESECVI monitoring framework is structurally distinctive globally — formal dedicated monitoring-body framework.

32 of 34 OAS member state party ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — most-widely-adopted regional binding-treaty framework on violence-against-women framework with near-universal Latin American + Caribbean adoption framework.
Cotton Field judgment 2009 landmark framework is structurally distinctive globally — first IACtHR judgment applying Belém do Pará Convention framework to state-party violations.

1979 CEDAW + 1994 Belém do Pará Convention + 1995 entry into force + 2004 MESECVI + 2009 Cotton Field + 2011 Istanbul Convention regional-women's-rights-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.
editorial_notes: Belém do Pará Convention jurisdiction sidecar — supranational Latin American regional violence-against-women binding-treaty meta-framework (Belém do Pará Convention 1994/1995 + MESECVI Establishment 2004 + Cotton Field 2009 + Veliz Franco 2014 + CEDAW 1979/1981). Only modern regional binding-treaty framework operating specifically on violence-against-women framework predating Istanbul Convention 2011 framework globally + central jurisdiction of formal Inter-American post-CEDAW comprehensive-violence-against-women framework elaboration + central jurisdiction of one of most distinctive modern Inter-American Court of Human Rights case-law frameworks + central jurisdiction of MESECVI monitoring framework + 32 of 34 OAS member state party ratification framework + Cotton Field judgment 2009 landmark framework + 1979 CEDAW + 1994 Belém do Pará Convention + 2004 MESECVI + 2009 Cotton Field + 2011 Istanbul Convention regional-women's-rights-framework-development sequence. Regional women's-rights triad complete (Belém do Pará 1994 + Istanbul Convention 2011 + CEDAW 1979 universal framework).
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins Latin-American + supranational + regional-violence-against-women-binding-treaty cluster + Belém-do-Pará-Convention-1994-first-regional-women-violence-binding-treaty-globally-distinctive + MESECVI-dedicated-monitoring-body + Cotton-Field-IACtHR-2009 + Veliz-Franco-IACtHR-2014 + 32-of-34-OAS-state-party-ratification + Istanbul-Convention-2011-Council-of-Europe-counterpart-framework + CEDAW-supranational-framework-underlying-1979-1981 + 17-year-predecessor-to-Istanbul-Convention clusters within the corpus.
